title: Morris Water Maze: Spatial Learning and Memory Assessment in Mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"emh56","text":"This protocol describes the Morris Water Maze (MWM) task for assessing hippocampus-dependent spatial","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9u627","text":"learning and memory (hidden-platform acquisition and long-term retention) and working memory in adult","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2epsl","text":"mice. Mice are trained to locate a hidden platform using distal spatial cues, with probe trials conducted at","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1v27l","text":"the end of each working week to assess memory consolidation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7htkb","text":"•       Maintain water temperature at 26 °C throughout each day of testing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"erri4","text":"•       Dry mice thoroughly between trials with a warm towel to prevent hypothermia.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7rrk","text":"•       Clean pool water regularly; replace ACUSOL‱ as needed to maintain opacity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":50,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"4sg92","text":"•       Exclude data for any trial in which the mouse floated or did not actively swim.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"19CDBB793D5A11F1A7890A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2ccdp","text":"• Prepare the pool: fill with 26 °C water and add ACUSOL‱ OP 301 at 0.5% until water is fully opaque.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":56,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"b5i8d","text":"• Position the hidden platform 1 cm below the water surface in a fixed quadrant (target quadrant).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3l3n6","text":"• Ensure distal spatial cues are available via differences in wall color and room structures (e.g., shelving,","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aaj20","text":"equipment). Do not rearrange the room during training.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fo3nu","text":"• Calibrate EthoVision tracking software: define four quadrants, record pool dimensions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"43b3r","text":"• Acclimate mice to the procedure room at least 30 min before testing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"BA7435CC3D8811F19BFC0A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}

steps: Training trials Each day, release each mouse from 4 different randomized starting positions (N, S, E, W quadrant edges). For each trial, allow up to 100 s to locate the hidden platform. Once on the platform, allow the mouse to remain for 15 s. If the mouse fails to locate the platform within 100 s, guide it to the platform and allow 15 s there. Return mouse to a holding cage with dry bedding for 15–30 min between trials. Starting positions should be randomized across animals and days to prevent position-based strategies.

Probe trials — end of each working week At the end of each working week, remove the hidden platform. Release the mouse from the opposite quadrant to the target. Allow free swimming for 100 s. Record the search pattern using EthoVision. A preference for the target quadrant indicates spatial memory consolidation.

Working memory version For the working memory version of the task, follow the same daily trial structure but reposition the hidden platform to a new fixed location at the start of each day. Probe trials assess within-day acquisition rather than long-term retention.

Data analysis EthoVision automatically computes the following variables: path length (cm), swim velocity (cm/s), and time spent in each zone/quadrant. Analyze escape latency and path length across training days (learning curve). For probe trials, compare time spent in the target quadrant vs. other quadrants. Apply appropriate statistical tests. WT mice should show a progressive reduction in escape latency across training days and significant preference for the target quadrant in probe trials (>25% time). Impaired spatial learning in KO mice would manifest as elevated escape latency and/or absence of quadrant preference.
creator: Sarah van Veen
